verdict_reasoning: GNAQ is a soluble cytoplasmic G protein alpha subunit coupled to 7TM receptor signaling. It associates with the cytoplasmic (inner) face of the plasma membrane via N-terminal lipidation (myristoylation) and transient interactions with receptor and effector complexes on the intracellular side. It lacks a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any other mechanism to reach the outer leaflet. The 7TM receptors it couples to are the surface proteins; GNAQ itself remains intracellular and inaccessible from outside the cell.
